verses.latin_vulgate: Hæ quoque parabolæ Salomonis, quas transtulerunt viri Ezechiæ regis Juda.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־אֵ֭לֶּה מִשְׁלֵ֣י שְׁלֹמֹ֑ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר הֶ֝עְתִּ֗יקוּ אַנְשֵׁ֤י ׀ חִזְקִיָּ֬ה מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.king_james: These are also proverbs of Solomon, which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gloria Dei est celare verbum,  et gloria regum investigare sermonem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּבֹ֣ד אֱ֭לֹהִים הַסְתֵּ֣ר דָּבָ֑ר וּכְבֹ֥ד מְ֝לָכִ֗ים חֲקֹ֣ר דָּבָֽר׃
verses.king_james: It is the glory of God to conceal a thing: but the honour of kings is to search out a matter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cælum sursum, et terra deorsum,  et cor regum inscrutabile.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁמַ֣יִם לָ֭רוּם וָאָ֣רֶץ לָעֹ֑מֶק וְלֵ֥ב מְ֝לָכִ֗ים אֵ֣ין חֵֽקֶר׃
verses.king_james: The heaven for height, and the earth for depth, and the heart of kings is unsearchable.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aufer rubiginem de argento,  et egredietur vas purissimum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָג֣וֹ סִיגִ֣ים מִכָּ֑סֶף וַיֵּצֵ֖א לַצֹּרֵ֣ף כֶּֽלִי׃
verses.king_james: Take away the dross from the silver, and there shall come forth a vessel for the finer.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aufer impietatem de vultu regis,  et firmabitur justitia thronus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָג֣וֹ רָ֭שָׁע לִפְנֵי־מֶ֑לֶךְ וְיִכּ֖וֹן בַּצֶּ֣דֶק כִּסְאֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Take away the wicked from before the king, and his throne shall be established in righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne gloriosus appareas coram rege,  et in loco magnorum ne steteris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תִּתְהַדַּ֥ר לִפְנֵי־מֶ֑לֶךְ וּבִמְק֥וֹם גְּ֝דֹלִ֗ים אַֽל־תַּעֲמֹֽד׃
verses.king_james: Put not forth thyself in the presence of the king, and stand not in the place of great men:

verses.latin_vulgate: Melius est enim ut dicatur tibi : Ascende huc,  quam ut humilieris coram principe.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י ט֥וֹב אֲמׇר־לְךָ֗ עֲֽלֵ֫ה־הֵ֥נָּה מֵ֭הַשְׁפִּ֣ילְךָ לִפְנֵ֣י נָדִ֑יב אֲשֶׁ֖ר רָא֣וּ עֵינֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: For better it is that it be said unto thee, Come up hither; than that thou shouldest be put lower in the presence of the prince whom thine eyes have seen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ viderunt oculi tui ne proferas in jurgio cito,  ne postea emendare non possis,  cum dehonestaveris amicum tuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תֵּצֵ֥א לָרִ֗ב מַ֫הֵ֥ר פֶּ֣ן מַה־תַּ֭עֲשֶׂה בְּאַחֲרִיתָ֑הּ בְּהַכְלִ֖ים אֹתְךָ֣ רֵעֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Go not forth hastily to strive, lest thou know not what to do in the end thereof, when thy neighbour hath put thee to shame.

verses.latin_vulgate: Causam tuam tracta cum amico tuo,  et secretum extraneo ne reveles :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רִ֭יבְךָ רִ֣יב אֶת־רֵעֶ֑ךָ וְס֖וֹד אַחֵ֣ר אַל־תְּגָֽל׃
verses.king_james: Debate thy cause with thy neighbour himself; and discover not a secret to another:

verses.latin_vulgate: ne forte insultet tibi cum audierit,  et exprobrare non cesset.  Gratia et amicitia liberant :  quas tibi serva, ne exprobrabilis fias.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פֶּֽן־יְחַסֶּדְךָ֥ שֹׁמֵ֑עַ וְ֝דִבָּתְךָ֗ לֹ֣א תָשֽׁוּב׃
verses.king_james: Lest he that heareth it put thee to shame, and thine infamy turn not away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mala aurea in lectis argenteis,  qui loquitur verbum in tempore suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּפּוּחֵ֣י זָ֭הָב בְּמַשְׂכִּיּ֣וֹת כָּ֑סֶף דָּ֝בָ֗ר דָּבֻ֥ר עַל־אׇפְנָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in pictures of silver.

verses.latin_vulgate: Inauris aurea, et margaritum fulgens,  qui arguit sapientem et aurem obedientem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֶ֣זֶם זָ֭הָב וַחֲלִי־כָ֑תֶם מוֹכִ֥יחַ חָ֝כָ֗ם עַל־אֹ֥זֶן שֹׁמָֽעַת׃
verses.king_james: As an earring of gold, and an ornament of fine gold, so is a wise reprover upon an obedient ear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut frigus nivis in die messis,  ita legatus fidelis ei qui misit eum :  animam ipsius requiescere facit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּצִנַּת־שֶׁ֨לֶג ׀ בְּי֬וֹם קָצִ֗יר צִ֣יר נֶ֭אֱמָן לְשֹׁלְחָ֑יו וְנֶ֖פֶשׁ אֲדֹנָ֣יו יָשִֽׁיב׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: As the cold of snow in the time of harvest, so is a faithful messenger to them that send him: for he refresheth the soul of his masters.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nubes, et ventus, et pluviæ non sequentes,  vir gloriosus et promissa non complens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נְשִׂיאִ֣ים וְ֭רוּחַ וְגֶ֣שֶׁם אָ֑יִן אִ֥ישׁ מִ֝תְהַלֵּ֗ל בְּמַתַּת־שָֽׁקֶר׃
verses.king_james: Whoso boasteth himself of a false gift is like clouds and wind without rain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Patientia lenietur princeps,  et lingua mollis confringet duritiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּאֹ֣רֶךְ אַ֭פַּיִם יְפֻתֶּ֣ה קָצִ֑ין וְלָשׁ֥וֹן רַ֝כָּ֗ה תִּשְׁבׇּר־גָּֽרֶם׃
verses.king_james: By long forbearing is a prince persuaded, and a soft tongue breaketh the bone.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mel invenisti : comede quod sufficit tibi,  ne forte satiatus evomas illud.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דְּבַ֣שׁ מָ֭צָאתָ אֱכֹ֣ל דַּיֶּ֑ךָּ פֶּן־תִּ֝שְׂבָּעֶ֗נּוּ וַהֲקֵאתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Hast thou found honey? eat so much as is sufficient for thee, lest thou be filled therewith, and vomit it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Subtrahe pedem tuum de domo proximi tui,  nequando satiatus oderit te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֹקַ֣ר רַ֭גְלְךָ מִבֵּ֣ית רֵעֶ֑ךָ פֶּן־יִ֝שְׂבָּעֲךָ֗ וּשְׂנֵאֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Withdraw thy foot from thy neighbour’s house; lest he be weary of thee, and so hate thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jaculum, et gladius, et sagitta acuta,  homo qui loquitur contra proximum suum falsum testimonium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵפִ֣יץ וְ֭חֶרֶב וְחֵ֣ץ שָׁנ֑וּן אִ֥ישׁ עֹנֶ֥ה בְ֝רֵעֵ֗הוּ עֵ֣ד שָֽׁקֶר׃
verses.king_james: A man that beareth false witness against his neighbour is a maul, and a sword, and a sharp arrow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dens putridus, et pes lassus,  qui sperat super infideli in die angustiæ,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֵׁ֣ן רֹ֭עָה וְרֶ֣גֶל מוּעָ֑דֶת מִבְטָ֥ח בּ֝וֹגֵ֗ד בְּי֣וֹם צָרָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Confidence in an unfaithful man in time of trouble is like a broken tooth, and a foot out of joint.

verses.latin_vulgate: et amittit pallium in die frigoris.  Acetum in nitro,  qui cantat carmina cordi pessimo.  Sicut tinea vestimento, et vermis ligno,  ita tristitia viri nocet cordi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַ֥עֲדֶה־בֶּ֨גֶד ׀ בְּי֣וֹם קָ֭רָה חֹ֣מֶץ עַל־נָ֑תֶר וְשָׁ֥ר בַּ֝שִּׁרִ֗ים עַ֣ל לֶב־רָֽע׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: As he that taketh away a garment in cold weather, and as vinegar upon nitre, so is he that singeth songs to an heavy heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si esurierit inimicus tuus, ciba illum ;  si sitierit, da ei aquam bibere :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־רָעֵ֣ב שֹׂ֭נַאֲךָ הַאֲכִלֵ֣הוּ לָ֑חֶם וְאִם־צָ֝מֵ֗א הַשְׁקֵ֥הוּ מָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: If thine enemy be hungry, give him bread to eat; and if he be thirsty, give him water to drink:

verses.latin_vulgate: prunas enim congregabis super caput ejus,  et Dominus reddet tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י גֶחָלִ֗ים אַ֭תָּה חֹתֶ֣ה עַל־רֹאשׁ֑וֹ וַ֝יהֹוָ֗ה יְשַׁלֶּם־לָֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: For thou shalt heap coals of fire upon his head, and the Lord shall reward thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ventus aquilo dissipat pluvias,  et facies tristis linguam detrahentem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ר֣וּחַ צָ֭פוֹן תְּח֣וֹלֵֽל גָּ֑שֶׁם וּפָנִ֥ים נִ֝זְעָמִ֗ים לְשׁ֣וֹן סָֽתֶר׃
verses.king_james: The north wind driveth away rain: so doth an angry countenance a backbiting tongue.

verses.latin_vulgate: Melius est sedere in angulo domatis  quam cum muliere litigiosa et in domo communi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ט֗וֹב שֶׁ֥בֶת עַל־פִּנַּת־גָּ֑ג מֵאֵ֥שֶׁת (מדונים) [מִ֝דְיָנִ֗ים] וּבֵ֥ית חָֽבֶר׃
verses.king_james: It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman and in a wide house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aqua frigida animæ sitienti,  et nuntius bonus de terra longinqua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַ֣יִם קָ֭רִים עַל־נֶ֣פֶשׁ עֲיֵפָ֑ה וּשְׁמוּעָ֥ה ט֝וֹבָ֗ה מֵאֶ֥רֶץ מֶרְחָֽק׃
verses.king_james: As cold waters to a thirsty soul, so is good news from a far country.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fons turbatus pede et vena corrupta,  justus cadens coram impio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַעְיָ֣ן נִ֭רְפָּשׂ וּמָק֣וֹר מׇשְׁחָ֑ת צַ֝דִּ֗יק מָ֣ט לִפְנֵֽי־רָשָֽׁע׃
verses.king_james: A righteous man falling down before the wicked is as a troubled fountain, and a corrupt spring.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut qui mel multum comedit non est ei bonum,  sic qui scrutator est majestatis opprimetur a gloria.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֘כֹ֤ל דְּבַ֣שׁ הַרְבּ֣וֹת לֹא־ט֑וֹב וְחֵ֖קֶר כְּבֹדָ֣ם כָּבֽוֹד׃
verses.king_james: It is not good to eat much honey: so for men to search their own glory is not glory.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut urbs patens et absque murorum ambitu,  ita vir qui non potest in loquendo cohibere spiritum suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עִ֣יר פְּ֭רוּצָה אֵ֣ין חוֹמָ֑ה אִ֝֗ישׁ אֲשֶׁ֤ר אֵ֖ין מַעְצָ֣ר לְרוּחֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: He that hath no rule over his own spirit is like a city that is broken down, and without walls.
